name: CelloCad
function: Automatically designs genetic circuit DNA sequences from high-level logic specifications written in Verilog (a hardware description language)
input: Verilog code (.v), User Constraint File (UCF) defining available genetic gates
output: DNA sequence layout (SBOL or GenBank format)
organism_scope: Primarily E. coli (with modern expansions to mammalian cells and other bacterial hosts)

name: iBioSim
function: Models, analyzes and dynamically simulates genetic circuits, metabolic networks, and cell-signaling pathways
output: Dynamic simulation data, ODE/stochastic plots, abstract system models

name: SynBioHub
function: A public design repository and registry that allows synthetic biologists to securely store, share, and track genetic parts and circuit designs
output: Standardized SBOL metadata, sequence files, and visual part maps

name: COPASI
function: A comprehensive biochemical network simulator that models reaction kinetics using ODEs, stochastic math and steady-state calculations
input: SBML files, biochemical equations, rate parameters

name: BSim
function: An agent-based simulator designed to model the spatial movements, constraints, and behavioral interactions of dense bacterial populations in 3D
input: Java configuration scripts defining cell behavior and environmental physical parameters
output: 3D spatial simulation renderings, cell-tracking vectors, population growth dynamics

name: Tellurium
function: A Python-based environment designed for reproducible, dynamic modeling, simulation, and parameter optimization of biological networks
input: Antimony strings (human-readable modeling text), SBML models, SED-ML experiment instructions
output: Simulation plots, numerical data arrays, COMBINE archives

name: RBS Calculator (Salis lab)
function: Predicts the translation initiation rate of a ribosome binding site sequence, or engineers a custom sequence to match a desired translation rate
input: Target mRNA sequence, organism translation characteristics, target initiation rate
output: Optimized DNA/RNA sequence, calculated translation initiation rates
license: free for academics / paid for commercial

name: SBOL Visual
function: A universally accepted standard graphical language that provides structured biological glyphs to visualize genetic circuits consistently
input: N/A (It is a glyph library specification standard ingested by structural drawing utilities).
output: Standardized vector graphics (promoters, terminators, barcodes, etc.).
interface: web app / integrated into external tools

name: Pigeon
function: A web and command-line application that quickly translates simple, shorthand text scripts into high-quality, SBOL Visual-compliant genetic circuit diagram
input: Plaintext shorthand string instructions (e.g., specifying promoters, coding zones, and color codes).
output: Genetic circuit image diagrams (SVG, PNG, or PDF formats).
